feedback.text: Recently there's been a lot of talk about digital goods (specifically videogames) not being an owned product after purchase, which lets some companies (for example Ubisoft, a French company) get away with taking consumers' money to later take away access to products that have been bought (such as their videogame The Crew). Because of this, citizens of EU feel like they are being robbed, considering that not every aspect of a purchased product requires online connection in order to access it. It would be good to see an initiative such as this one to take into consideration the value of digital purchases, and to help future titles not be taken away from paying customers. Especially since currently, the best treatment is given ironically to those who access said titles illegally, as they seem to be the only ones who actually own the games they download. It would help alot with legal purchases if products were permanent in the customer's hands, instead of what we've oddly grown to accept as the norm.
